item.title: Admin Post - Disciplinary - Open
item.content:encoded:
friskynisky, if you believe an article should use a certain formatting element, contact the author directly via wikidot PM.

Give how many pages you can asked the same question on, this is an official warning. Please do not spam comments asking to add alternate formatting to pages.

item.title: Acs?
item.content:encoded: Can I add an ACS heading?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this a lot, though part of me wishes it took the concept a little further; what if the file name or the little folder logo was physically visible on the cloud, and changed with additions? Even as it is, it's great, and the little twist with the storm at the end brings it home.

many thanks for addition to fine catlog

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
4 do not use when in rain or storm or oh no, file lost and get wet.

Well, after literal square roots of grizzly bear I'm not surprised to see a file literally getting wet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is actually the stupidest part of it: He actually meant horse.

There's a horse called Cloud Computing. When I found out when editing, I had to add that in as a clue to just how confused dado had been by words

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe "worse" ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and not false advertisement or horse.

Horse? What did he intend to say here?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: dado

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is just so weird and it's perfect for dado.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: some say cloud not a cloud, just someone else's computer. NOT here. dado cloud is cloud, yes. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better. The dado-speak isn't perfect (I suggest tinkering with it based on how you see him talk in some of the other popular dado articles. SCP-4949 comes to mind based on the advertisement and product disclaimer stuff you need to include.), but it is much better than it was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for taking a peak!

I will admit my writing has a very 'why use one word when you can use 15 instead' quantity to it. The original idea for this had two instances of the dado letter, which I quashed well before it hit draft but unfortunately, yeah, bit of its shadow still creeps across

I'll give it a grind when I get a chance in the next few days and see if I can rework it to something better

EDIT: Naturally I work on the one day of the year its practically melting. I've condensed the dado portions down, how's that look to you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a sucker for dado, but there are a few things off about his tone here.

thank u 4 purchase of fine dado goods. dado is undergo many hardship at moment but dado is hero of capitalism. dado is undergo restructure, and to show dado trust of many good customer like u, he provide to u with secret dado product no intended for stores. this is look into dado process that show that just like u trust dado, dado 2 trusts u yes

u may ask, dado, why u make computer disk program? dado is tell you now. dado go 2 store one day and see much noise about cloud compute. hmm! dado think this be big opportunity 4 business and so dado go and talk to discuss improving of system and how this can help.

This in particular is less of a "dado-speak", and more of a "first grader with a crayon" type of writing. The best thing about dado dialogue is that he comes off in a way that makes him seem like an imitation of a person, and not an actual person. It should feel like dado understands humanity, but isn't perfect at replicating that himself. He isn't ever incoherent and babbling like he does in this note. I personally think that the article would be much better if the whole dado note was trimmed down to like 3 or 4 lines maximum. I keep thinking back to Youth in Asia by dado, which was another dado article not written by kak, and how that article perfectly represents dado's speech patterns. Dado doesn't get long-winded. He speaks in short, single-clause sentences with slightly incorrect grammar. Try to emulate that in your writings of him.

That being said, I still enjoyed myself, even if the dado-speak was seriously lacking. Tentative +1 since i trust dado. Novote until an edit comes this way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Im gonna concur with nicolini here. tTe concept is great, but the execution is a little rough. I do like dado and think this is a good idea but the article just doesn't sell it for me, so novote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Always up for any feedback or criticism if anyone has anything to post! It was positively received over in Drafts, but I also understand dado is a bit of a ah…. Love it or hate it subject

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
hello am dado say thank to foundation for use dado cloud yes. dado cloud store no scp, dado cloud helpful product for buy
A holy first SCP +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dado love new scp yes

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
great idea, not so great execution. I'm also not a fan of 'note at the end' trope, but that's more of a 'me' thing.
If you got some extra crit on this and polished it some more I would happily upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i am

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whos downvoting this its great

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would love criticism if anyone has any problems with it or issues. I hunger for it, even.

An earlier draft I had of this specified the time it took for files to transfer was based on filesize, hence why they were being treated as such. So the 1.2 tb file went instantly (Well, 5 seconds), and the 1 kb file took 2 minutes-ish to send due to its filename.

It got chopped out during editing at some point which I just noticed, my apologies. Reckon I should slap that in for clarity?
EDIT: And the second I turn my computer off I immediately get an idea on how to put it in better. Adjusted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
2 open 'cloud.dado' yes

the yes gets me every time

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
how this get many down boats? is fine dado article about the dado cloud compute!

i don't understand part about file size and file name, this is fine mystery but i trust author will help? thank you yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have many criticisms but the concept itself carries this for me - that’s extremely rare.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean yes this is very very good for me thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One's first SCP is the culmination of their efforts thus far in terms of the Foundation. The culmination of all of the articles and tales they've read so far, soaking into their being. Its an expression of love, of unity, of wanting to be a part of that website that's given them so much thus far, and wanting to make their mark on it, hoping it lasts for all time.

I wrote a dado article as my first one so what that says about me, I don't want to speculate

Extra special thanks to RockTeethMothEyes and djkaktus for reviewing my draft, and of course, to djkaktus again for inventing dado!
